meta.title: Bret Taylor of Sierra on AI Agents, Outcome-Based Pricing, and the OpenAI Board

questions.question: According to the outline, what is an agent harness?
options: A protocol for exposing tools to agents, similar to MCP
The context, skills, documentation, and rules given to an AI agent so it can act effectively
A type of outcome-based pricing model used by Sierra
The database that serves as the system of record for a department
questions.answer: The context, skills, documentation, and rules given to an AI agent so it can act effectively

questions.question: Why does Taylor argue outcome-based pricing differs from usage-based pricing?
options: Usage-based is illegal in enterprise software
Token use is not well correlated with business value; outcomes align with the result the client cares about
Outcome-based pricing only works for customer service, not sales
Usage-based pricing requires more compliance documentation
questions.answer: Token use is not well correlated with business value; outcomes align with the result the client cares about

questions.question: In Sierra's outcome-based pricing model for customer service, what typically happens when a case is escalated to a human?
options: The client pays a premium rate for human handling
Sierra charges per minute of human time
That escalation is free for the client
The client must switch to a usage-based contract
questions.answer: That escalation is free for the client

questions.question: Why is coding described as a particularly favorable domain for AI agents?
options: Coding pays the highest rates for outcome-based pricing
Context is in one place (textual files), with feedback from tests, version history, and code review
Coding was the first domain to adopt MCP
Software engineers were the first to use OpenClaw
questions.answer: Context is in one place (textual files), with feedback from tests, version history, and code review

questions.question: What problem does the outline identify with multi-agent designs where subagents hold context but the orchestrator does not?
options: Subagents become too expensive to run
The orchestrator has no ability to sound non-robotic or understand what is behind the APIs
MCP does not support multiple agents
It violates data privacy regulations
questions.answer: The orchestrator has no ability to sound non-robotic or understand what is behind the APIs

questions.question: What is the supervisor or constellation-of-models pattern used by Sierra?
options: Multiple agents compete and the best answer is selected
A second model inspects and corrects the first (e.g., forcing lookups), improving reliability
One model writes code while another runs tests
Agents take turns handling different channels (phone, chat)
questions.answer: A second model inspects and corrects the first (e.g., forcing lookups), improving reliability

questions.question: What does Taylor give as the "atomic unit of productivity" when thinking about AI?
options: The person
The department
The process
The system of record

questions.question: Why might well-known brands be harder to ground in an AI agent than obscure ones?
options: Well-known brands have more data and overload the model
The LLM may answer off the top of its head instead of looking up the brand's actual policies
Obscure brands use simpler APIs
Well-known brands require more MCP servers
questions.answer: The LLM may answer off the top of its head instead of looking up the brand's actual policies

questions.question: According to the outline, which type of system of record does Taylor suggest is more durable when agents perform labor?
options: Systems of engagement (e.g., CRM, marketing tools)
Systems of record that are essentially ledgers (e.g., ERP for finance)
Systems built on MCP
Systems that use outcome-based pricing
questions.answer: Systems of record that are essentially ledgers (e.g., ERP for finance)

questions.question: What is the Jevons paradox example given in the outline?
options: Sierra's ARR grew faster after raising prices
A retailer's conversation volume went up almost as much as savings when they replaced a bad chatbot with a delightful agent
OpenClaw used fewer tokens than ChatGPT for the same task
Rocket Mortgage reduced headcount while growing market share
questions.answer: A retailer's conversation volume went up almost as much as savings when they replaced a bad chatbot with a delightful agent

questions.question: When did Sierra launch?
options: February 13, 2024
October 2025
March 2026

questions.question: Taylor's fiduciary duty on the OpenAI board is described as different from a typical corporate board because it is a duty to what?
options: Maximize shareholder value
Ensure artificial general intelligence benefits humanity
Represent the interests of the largest model lab
Approve all product launches
questions.answer: Ensure artificial general intelligence benefits humanity

questions.question: Who was cited as wanting to "SSH into your Stripe account" (CLI, tail, pipe)?
options: Bret Taylor
Dario Amodei
Patrick (Collison)
Varun

questions.question: Why does the outline say companies "ship our org charts" and how does that affect AI productivity?
options: Org charts are required for compliance in financial services
There is usually no single person accountable for an end-to-end process, so process optimization is harder
AI agents need an org chart to know who to escalate to
Department heads refuse to share data with other departments
questions.answer: There is usually no single person accountable for an end-to-end process, so process optimization is harder

questions.question: What kind of people are described as "massively ascendant" with AI, gaining an "exoskeleton"?
options: Specialists who know one domain in great depth
High-agency, customer-caring people who may not have been the strongest engineers
Managers who focus on two-pizza teams
Engineers who write the most lines of code
questions.answer: High-agency, customer-caring people who may not have been the strongest engineers

questions.question: In the 1994 analogy about websites and banks, what is the correct framing for competitive technology adoption?
options: Launching a website gave banks a lasting competitive advantage
If you don't launch a website, you fall behind—so adoption is an imperative, not a competitive advantage
Banks that launched websites first could charge higher fees
Only one bank needed to launch a website per region
questions.answer: If you don't launch a website, you fall behind—so adoption is an imperative, not a competitive advantage

questions.question: Why does Taylor say applied AI has enduring value even if models improve ("short AGI" concern)?
options: Applied AI companies will acquire the model labs
Companies buy solutions and purpose-built product for their buyers and processes, not raw models; product, GTM, and ecosystem matter
Token costs will rise and make raw models uneconomical
Regulation will require using applied AI vendors only
questions.answer: Companies buy solutions and purpose-built product for their buyers and processes, not raw models; product, GTM, and ecosystem matter

questions.question: In the healthcare example, how do AI agents from different organizations (e.g., payer and provider) communicate?
options: Via a custom agent protocol built by Sierra
Using MCP servers hosted by each company
English over the public switched telephone network (PSTN)
Through a shared vector database
questions.answer: English over the public switched telephone network (PSTN)
